articles.headline: Kane eyes Lewandowski record: Tracking Europe's major goal, assist records
articles.description: Harry Kane is chasing Robert Lewandowski's incredible Bundesliga goal tally, while Kylian Mbappé seeks Champions League history. Here's the latest in Europe's record goal and assist tallies.

images.name: Harry Kane [1296x729]
images.caption: Kane scored his 20th Bundesliga goal of the season today after just 16 games.
images.credit: Photo by Tom Weller/picture alliance via Getty Images

images.name: Manchester United's Bruno Fernandes [1296x729]
images.caption: Bruno Fernandes' assist tally took a sizeable leap after Ruben Amorim was sacked as Manchester United manager.

images.name: Lamine Yamal, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Neither Kylian Mbappé nor Lamine Yamal are likely to break Lionel Messi's single-season goal and assist marks in LaLiga.

images.name: Inter Milan's Lautaro Martinez [1296x729]
images.caption: Lautaro Martinez easily leads the Seire A scoring chart, but he won't get near the single-season record.

images.name: Mason Greenwood aug 2025 [1296x729]
images.caption: Mason Greenwood was made to share the Ligue 1 top scorer title last season.

images.name: Vinicus Junior, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Kylian Mbappé is within reach of Cristiano Ronaldo's single-season Champions League goal record.
images.credit: David S. Bustamante/Real Madrid via Getty Images

images.name: Bayern Munich holds on for 3-2 win
images.caption: Harry Kane scores a brace as Bayern Munich beat Eintracht Frankfurt 3-2.

articles.headline: Transfer rumors, news: Man United keeping tabs on Stuttgart's Andrés
articles.description: Manchester United could look to the Bundesliga for their newest midfield signing. Transfer Talk has the latest.

images.name: Chema Andrés [1296x729]
images.credit: (Photo by Harry Langer/picture alliance via Getty Images)

images.name: Is Lamine Yamal the best player in the world?
images.caption: The 'ESPN FC' crew react to Lamine Yamal becoming the youngest player to score a LaLiga hat-trick this century as Barcelona beat Villarreal 4-1.

articles.headline: Arsenal win ugly vs. Chelsea; Bayern close in on Bundesliga title; more
articles.description: Arsenal muscled past Chelsea on Sunday, but coach Mikel Arteta can't be happy at how difficult things look for his team. Also: Bayern Munich likely wrapped up the Bundesliga with a Der Klassiker win.

images.name: Arsenal celeb v Chelsea 260302 [1296x729]
images.credit: Stuart MacFarlane/Arsenal FC via Getty Images

images.name: Can Arsenal ride set-pieces all the way to the title?
images.caption: Shaka Hislop and Steve Nicol wonder if Arsenal are too reliant on set-pieces to win the Premier League title.

keyEvents.text: Goal!  Borussia Dortmund 1, FC Augsburg 0. Youssoufa Moukoko (Borussia Dortmund) left footed shot from very close range to the bottom right corner.
Maximilian Bauer (FC Augsburg) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal!  Borussia Dortmund 2, FC Augsburg 0. Donyell Malen (Borussia Dortmund) header from very close range to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Jamie Bynoe-Gittens with a cross.

Goal!  Borussia Dortmund 3, FC Augsburg 0. Youssoufa Moukoko (Borussia Dortmund) right footed shot from very close range to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Marco Reus with a cross   following a corner.
Goal!  Borussia Dortmund 3, FC Augsburg 1. Rubén Vargas (FC Augsburg) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Arne Maier   following a corner.
Goal!  Borussia Dortmund 4, FC Augsburg 1. Marco Reus (Borussia Dortmund) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Kjell-Arik Wätjen with a through ball.
First Half ends, Borussia Dortmund 4, FC Augsburg 1.
Substitution, Borussia Dortmund. Nico Schlotterbeck replaces Mateu Morey.
Substitution, Borussia Dortmund. Karim Adeyemi replaces Donyell Malen.
Substitution, Borussia Dortmund. Salih Özcan replaces Marius Wolf.
Goal!  Borussia Dortmund 5, FC Augsburg 1. Felix Nmecha (Borussia Dortmund) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Marco Reus.
Substitution, Borussia Dortmund. Julian Brandt replaces Marco Reus.
Substitution, FC Augsburg. Niklas Dorsch replaces Rubén Vargas.
Substitution, FC Augsburg. Dion Beljo replaces Phillip Tietz.
Substitution, FC Augsburg. Patric Pfeiffer replaces Maximilian Bauer.
Substitution, FC Augsburg. Mert Kömür replaces Arne Maier.
Substitution, Borussia Dortmund. Marcel Sabitzer replaces Felix Nmecha.
Substitution, FC Augsburg. Arne Engels replaces Tim Breithaupt.
Second Half ends, Borussia Dortmund 5, FC Augsburg 1.

article.story:
Borussia Dortmund demolished Augsburg 5-1 in the Bundesliga on Saturday in a perfect dress rehearsal ahead of next week's Champions League semifinal second leg at Paris Saint-Germain.

Dortmund, who face PSG on Tuesday after their 1-0 first leg win in Germany earlier this week, enjoyed a whirlwind start with three goals in the first 29 minutes.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

Youssoufa Moukoko struck either side of Doneyll Malen's 20th minute goal to quickly put the game to bed.

Augsburg cut the deficit in the 32nd minute with Ruben Vargas but the visitors restored their three-goal cushion with Marco Reus, who will leave the club after 12 years at the end of the season, in the 34th minute.

He then delivered his second assist of the evening for Felix Nmecha to make it 5-1 in the 64th.

Dortmund, who have secured a Champions League spot for next season, are in fifth place on 60 points, three behind fourth-placed RB Leipzig.
